issues.title: Multiple <h1> headings

issues.recommendation: Use one <h1> per page and structure the rest with <h2>-<h6>.

issues.title: Hidden content that may need screen reader access
issues.description: Content hidden with CSS may still need to be announced.
issues.recommendation: Use aria-hidden="true" for decorative hidden content, or ensure it's accessible via keyboard.

issues.description: Content hidden with CSS may still need to be announced.
issues.recommendation: Use aria-hidden="true" for decorative hidden content, or ensure it's accessible via keyboard.

issues.title: ARIA role without accessible name
issues.description: Elements with ARIA roles must have an accessible name.
issues.recommendation: Add text content or aria-label to elements with ARIA roles.

issues.title: ARIA role without accessible name
issues.description: Elements with ARIA roles must have an accessible name.
issues.recommendation: Add text content or aria-label to elements with ARIA roles.

issues.title: ARIA role without accessible name
issues.description: Elements with ARIA roles must have an accessible name.
issues.recommendation: Add text content or aria-label to elements with ARIA roles.

issues.title: ARIA role without accessible name
issues.description: Elements with ARIA roles must have an accessible name.
issues.recommendation: Add text content or aria-label to elements with ARIA roles.

issues.title: Image used for text content
issues.description: Using images instead of text can cause scaling and maintenance problems.
issues.recommendation: Use CSS and HTML text formatting instead of images for text content.

issues.title: No client-side error handling detected
issues.description: Forms should provide suggestions when errors occur.
issues.recommendation: Implement client-side and server-side validation with helpful error messages and correction suggestions.

issues.title: Inconsistent link text for same destination
issues.description: Same destination referenced with different link text.
issues.recommendation: Use consistent link text for the same destination across the website.

issues.title: Vague link text found
issues.description: 16 link(s) use non-descriptive text like "click here" or "more".
issues.recommendation: Write descriptive link text that describes destination/action. Avoid "click here".

	img.wp-smiley, img.emoji {
		display: inline !important;
		border: none !important;
		box-shadow: none !important;
		height: 1em !important;
		width: 1em !important;

		background: none !important;
		padding: 0 !important;

.wp-block-co-authors-plus-coauthors.is-layout-flow [class*=wp-block-co-authors-plus]{display:inline}

</style>
<style id='co-authors-plus-avatar-style-inline-css' type='text/css'>
.wp-block-co-authors-plus-avatar :where(img){height:auto;max-width:100%;vertical-align:bottom}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-coauthors.is-layout-flow .wp-block-co-authors-plus-avatar :where(img){vertical-align:middle}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-avatar:is(.alignleft,.alignright){display:table}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-avatar.aligncenter{display:table;margin-inline:auto}

</style>
<style id='co-authors-plus-image-style-inline-css' type='text/css'>
.wp-block-co-authors-plus-image{margin-bottom:0}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-image :where(img){height:auto;max-width:100%;vertical-align:bottom}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-coauthors.is-layout-flow .wp-block-co-authors-plus-image :where(img){vertical-align:middle}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-image:is(.alignfull,.alignwide) :where(img){width:100%}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-image:is(.alignleft,.alignright){display:table}.wp-block-co-authors-plus-image.aligncenter{display:table;margin-inline:auto}

/* Popup Theme 128791: Hello Box */
.pum-theme-128791, .pum-theme-hello-box { background-color: rgba( 0, 0, 0, 0.75 ) }

<div id="a11y-modal" class="reveal large" title="Accessibility Statement" data-reveal>
	<textarea rows="21" class="statement-textarea" readonly>
This website adopts the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.0) as the accessibility standard for all its related web development and services. WCAG 2.0 is also an international standard, ISO 40500. This certifies it as a stable and referenceable technical standard. 

WCAG 2.0 contains 12 guidelines organized under 4 principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust (POUR for short). There are testable success criteria for each guideline. Compliance to these criteria is measured in three levels: A, AA, or AAA. A guide to understanding and implementing Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.0 is available at: https://www.w3.org/TR/UNDERSTANDING-WCAG20/

Accessibility Features

Shortcut Keys Combination Activation Combination keys used for each browser.

	Chrome for Linux press (Alt+Shift+shortcut_key) 
	Chrome for Windows press (Alt+shortcut_key) 
	For Firefox press (Alt+Shift+shortcut_key) 
	For Internet Explorer press (Alt+Shift+shortcut_key) then press (enter)
	On Mac OS press (Ctrl+Opt+shortcut_key)

	Accessibility Statement (Combination + 0): Statement page that will show the available accessibility keys. 
	Home Page (Combination + H): Accessibility key for redirecting to homepage. 
	Main Content (Combination + R): Shortcut for viewing the content section of the current page. 
	FAQ (Combination + Q): Shortcut for FAQ page. 
	Contact (Combination + C): Shortcut for contact page or form inquiries. 
	Feedback (Combination + K): Shortcut for feedback page. 
	Site Map (Combination + M): Shortcut for site map (footer agency) section of the page. 
	Search (Combination + S): Shortcut for search page. 

Press esc, or click the close the button to close this dialog box.
	</textarea>

				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
							<p><div class="pt-cv-wrapper"><div class="pt-cv-view pt-cv-grid pt-cv-colsys" id="pt-cv-view-60dadeei3c"><div data-id="pt-cv-page-1" class="pt-cv-page" data-cvc="2"><div class="col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 pt-cv-content-item pt-cv-1-col" ><div class='pt-cv-ifield'><h3 class="pt-cv-title"><a href="https://www.deped.gov.ph/2026/03/09/on-the-death-of-a-learner-from-taguig-city/" class="_self" target="_self" >On the Death of a Learner from Taguig City</a></h3>
<div class="pt-cv-content">March 09, 2026 — The Department of Education (DepEd) extends its deepest condolences to the family and friends of a learner from Taguig City who tragically lost his life in light of a personal and sensitive incident. In response, DepEd promptly mobilized its mental health crisis response mechanisms. <script>
jQuery(document).ready(function($) {
  // Handle download button click
  document.querySelectorAll('.download-btn').forEach(function (btn) {
    btn.addEventListener('click', function () {
      const fileUrl = btn.getAttribute('data-download-url');
      sessionStorage.setItem('selectedFile', fileUrl);

      // Check if survey already submitted in this session
      if (sessionStorage.getItem('surveySubmitted') === 'true') {
        // Directly download the file
        const link = document.createElement('a');
        link.href = fileUrl;
        link.download = '';
        document.body.appendChild(link);
        link.click();
        document.body.removeChild(link);
      } else {
        // Show agreement popup
        PUM.open(141094); // Agreement Popup ID
      }
    });
  });

  // Wait for agreement popup to load and bind continue button
  const waitForAgreement = setInterval(function () {
    const continueBtn = document.getElementById('continue-button');
    const checkbox = document.getElementById('terms-checkbox');

    if (continueBtn && checkbox) {
      clearInterval(waitForAgreement);
      continueBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {
        if (checkbox.checked) {
           const fileUrl = sessionStorage.getItem('selectedFile');
            if (fileUrl) {
              sessionStorage.setItem('surveySubmitted', 'true'); // Prevent future popups
              const link = document.createElement('a');
              link.href = fileUrl;
              link.download = '';
              document.body.appendChild(link);
              link.click();
              document.body.removeChild(link);
            }
          PUM.close(141094); // Close Agreement Popup
          setTimeout(() => {
            PUM.open(141127); // Open Survey Popup
          }, 500);
        } else {
          alert("You must agree to the terms to continue.");
        }
      });
    }
  }, 100);

  // After survey submission, trigger download and set session flag
  $(document).on('frmFormComplete', function(event, form, response) {
    const fileUrl = sessionStorage.getItem('selectedFile');
    if (fileUrl) {
      sessionStorage.setItem('surveySubmitted', 'true'); // Prevent future popups
      const link = document.createElement('a');
      link.href = fileUrl;
      link.download = '';
      document.body.appendChild(link);
      link.click();
      document.body.removeChild(link);
    }
  });
});
</script>

<h3>Please read the full technical note on the limitations and disclaimers of the datasets:</h3>
<div class="myheader" style="text-align:center;">
<p style="margin-bottom:0 !important;">Terms of Use and Fair Use Policy</p>

<p style="padding-top: 15px;">By using these datasets, you agree to the following Terms and Conditions of Use and Fair Use Policy:</p>
<ol type="1">
<!-- 1. Restrictions on the Use of Materials --></p>
<li style="font-weight: bold; margin-left: 40px"> Restrictions on the Use of Materials</li>
<p style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 40px">The datasets, including documents (collectively referred to as “Materials”), are uploaded as “Information”  in accordance with Executive Order No. 2, 2016, titled <i>“Operationalizing in the Executive Branch the People’s Constitutional Right to Information and the State Policies of Full Public Disclosure and Transparency in the Public Service and Providing Guidelines Therefor.”</i></p>
<p style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 40px">You are free to <b>Share</b> – copy and redistribute the Materials in any medium or format and <b>Adapt</b> – reorganize, transform, and build upon the Materials in any medium or format for any purpose, under the following conditions:</p>
<ol type="a" style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 50px">
  <!-- a b c d--></p>
<li><b>Attribution</b> — You must give appropriate credit and acknowledgement to the Licensor — Department of Education (DepEd). As much as practicable, attribution may be done by providing a link to the license or in any reasonable manner, but not in any way that suggests the Licensor endorses you or your use.</li>
<p></p>
<li><b>Non-Modification</b> — The Materials should not and cannot be modified/edited except for editorial changes. Any request for modification/edit shall be approved by DepEd prior to publication. For such purposes, DepEd may be reached at <u><b>projectbukas.feedback@deped.gov.ph.</b></u></li>
<p></p>
<li><b>Redistribution and Derivative Works</b> – If you reorganize, transform, or build upon any of the Materials, you must distribute your contributions under the same license as the original. Any restructuring or derivative work must ensure the accuracy of the contents of the Material.</li>
<p></p>
<li><b>No Indication of Involvement, Endorsement, or Association</b> — By using the DepEd datasets and/or Materials, you must ensure that there is no indication that DepEd and any of its Authors have been involved, have contributed, or are associated with your work. The Materials cannot be used to endorse a position that is not aligned with the intention of the original authors. Furthermore, the name, trademark, official mark, official emblem, logo, or any insignia of DepEd shall not be used without its prior written consent.</li>
<p style="margin-left: 0px">______________________________</p>
<p style="font-size:13px; text-align: justify;margin-left: -10px"><sup>1</sup> Section 1 (a) “Information” shall mean any records, documents, papers, reports, letters, contracts, minutes and transcripts of official meetings, maps, books, photographs, data, research materials, films, sound and video recording, magnetic or other tapes, electronic data, computer stored data, any other like or similar data or materials recorded, stored or archived in whatever format, whether offline or online, which are made, received or kept in or under the control and custody of any government office pursuant to law, executive order, and rules and regulations or in connection with the performance or transaction of official business by any government office.</p>
<li><b>No additional restrictions</b> — You may not apply legal terms or technological measures that legally restrict others from doing anything the license permits.</li>

</ol>
<p><!-- 2. Right to Modify --></p>
<li style="font-weight: bold; margin-left: 40px"> Right to Modify</li>
<p style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 40px">DepEd reserves the right, at its own discretion, to update or modify this site and its materials at any time without notice.</p>
<p><!-- 3. Disclaimer of Warranties and Limitation of Liability --></p>
<li style="font-weight: bold; margin-left: 40px"> Disclaimer of Warranties and Limitation of Liability</li>
<p style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 40px">Unless otherwise separately undertaken by DepEd, to such extent as possible, DepEd offers the Licensed Material “as is” and “as available”, and makes no representations or warranties of any kind concerning the Licensed Materials, whether express, implied, statutory, or other. This includes, but is not limited to, warranties of title, merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, absence of latent or other defects, accuracy, or the presence or absence of errors, whether or not known or discoverable.</p>
<p style="text-align: justify;margin-left: 40px">In no event shall DepEd be liable to you based on any legal theory, including but not limited to negligence for any direct, special, indirect, incidental, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or other losses, costs, expenses, or damages arising out of the Licensed Materials.</p>
<p>
</ol>
<h4 style="margin-top: 30px;">Guidelines on Assessment Data Usage</h4>
<ol type="1" style="font-size: 1.1rem; margin-left: 40px">
<li> <b>No comparison of test scores across school years.</b> <span style="font-size: 17px; font-weight: 100 !important;">Test items can vary in difficulty from year to year, making it inaccurate to compare scores across different school years. Additionally, the schools and learners selected to take the tests may differ each year, which affects the modality of test administration, whether census or sampling. Consequently, differences in math or numeracy scores between 2017 and 2024 may not indicate an actual improvement in learning outcomes at the school. Instead, focus on the latest available mean percentage scores and proficiency levels for your school, division, or local government unit (LGU) to start understanding the factors influencing these scores.</span></li>
<p></p>
<li> <b>No Ranking of schools.</b> <span style="font-size: 17px; font-weight: 100 !important;">Schools operate within different contexts that can explain variations in learning outcomes. Factors contributing to these differences may include income, size and location of the school, learner-teacher or classroom-teacher ratios, access to resources and funding, teaching and learning delivery methods, and the size of the student population. Rather than ranking schools, compare test scores to different benchmarks: the national average, regional average, or division average mean percentage scores (MPS) in certain subjects, or proficiency levels.</span></li>
</ol>
